_comment: CIL Explorer — Data Schema Reference. Use this as the template when populating norms in Phase 2.

norm_template.title: Plain-English statement of the norm
norm_template.domain: One of: armed_conflict | human_rights | state_responsibility | diplomatic_consular | law_of_the_sea | environment | use_of_force | jurisdiction_immunity
norm_template.contested: Boolean — true if norm has significant dissenting state practice or academic dispute
norm_template.summary: 150-word description: rule statement → state practice evidence → opinio juris evidence → key disputes
norm_template.primary_codification: Closest treaty article, ILC rule, or ICJ case

state_practice.description: Brief description of the state act or omission

opinio_juris.description: Brief description of the legal belief demonstrated
opinio_juris.evidence_type: One of: un_resolution | treaty_preamble | national_legislation | judicial_statement | official_statement | military_manual

sources.type: One of: icj_judgment | icj_advisory | icrc_rule | un_resolution | treaty | national_legislation | arbitral_award | scholarly_article | military_manual | national_court
sources.title: Full citation title
sources.notes: Optional — flag if source is disputed or represents minority view

use_of_force.label: Use of force / jus ad bellum

status_definitions.established: Broad state acceptance, consistent practice, confirmed by ICJ or equivalent
status_definitions.emerging: Growing acceptance but practice not yet uniform; contested by significant minority of states
status_definitions.contested: Actively disputed — competing state practice or explicit disagreement among legal authorities

evidentiary_type_definitions.practice: Source demonstrates what states DO — acts, omissions, physical conduct
evidentiary_type_definitions.juris: Source demonstrates what states BELIEVE is legally required — opinio juris
evidentiary_type_definitions.both: Source serves as evidence of both elements simultaneously
